agency: NYC Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). MOCJ is a split domain: a thin informational stub on nyc.gov/site/criminaljustice (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' + Dynatrace/mPulse, most subpages 404) and the real office site at criminaljustice.cityofnewyork.us, fingerprinted as WordPress on WP Engine behind Cloudflare (x-powered-by: WP Engine; Kadence blocks; ACF; Contact Form 7; The Events Calendar; Yoast; Site Kit by Google). Enumerated the site's custom post types via its accidental wp/v2 REST API (programs 26, solicitation 25, notice 26, reports 26, data_reports 109, briefs 54, data_stories 6, team 92). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ)' via the Socrata Discovery API — it returns exactly ONE dataset (Supervised Release Dockets, atne-2dki). Read the System Data page: MOCJ analyzes jail-population/re-arrest as PDF explainers and links out to DOC/NYPD/DCJS/BOC for the underlying numbers.
headline_findings: MOCJ is a split domain: a thin nyc.gov/site/criminaljustice stub (NYC.gov Livesite) whose subpages 404, and the real office site at criminaljustice.cityofnewyork.us — WordPress on WP Engine behind Cloudflare, a fifth distinct platform after Parks' Smarty/PHP, DOE's Sitefinity/.NET, Council's WordPress, and NYCHA's Livesite+Siebel.

It is a policy/convening/grant-making office that OWNS almost no data: NYC Open Data carries exactly ONE MOCJ dataset — Supervised Release Dockets (atne-2dki, 6 columns, last updated 2023, ~1,074 views).

The jail-population numbers MOCJ is best known for are not MOCJ's. Its System Data page publishes PDF 'explainers' (jail-population increase, re-arrest rates) and links OUT to DOC (Daily Population in Custody), NYPD (CompStat), NYS DCJS, and the Board of Correction. MOCJ convenes and analyzes; it does not own the metrics.

The only machine-readable surface MOCJ has is ACCIDENTAL: the WordPress wp/v2 REST API, which exposes programs, solicitations, notices, reports, briefs, and data stories as JSON — undocumented, unintended, and not a product. A Contact Form 7 REST endpoint is the only write path, and it is a generic web form.

Net-new write surface: a ProgramReferral — routing a person or court case to a MOCJ-coordinated program along the Sequential Intercept Model. This is the office's core function, yet it has no API; referrals happen offline or via a generic web form. Referral data is sensitive and must be gated. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

meta.platform: WordPress (WP Engine) behind Cloudflare; Kadence/ACF/Contact Form 7 + accidental wp/v2 REST API
meta.netnew: ProgramReferral — route a person or court case to a MOCJ-coordinated program
meta.tagline: A coordination office that owns almost no data and routes people between courts, agencies, and community programs with PDFs and outbound links — the fix is to give that routing a machine-readable contract.
meta.headline: MOCJ convenes and analyzes but owns almost nothing — one stale dataset, an accidental WordPress API, and jail numbers that belong to other agencies — so the modernization move is to route its programs, publications, and referrals through one owned API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Managed WordPress (WP Engine) + Cloudflare + Kadence/ACF — a competent marketing CMS, wrong tool for a data/coordination mission.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: Accidental but functional WordPress wp/v2 REST API exposes programs/reports/solicitations as JSON; undocumented, unintended, not a product.
scorecard_notes.openData: Exactly one dataset (Supervised Release Dockets, 6 cols), unchanged since 2023; near-absence.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core criminal-justice metrics live in PDF explainers and other agencies' dashboards, not as data.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Only generic Contact Form 7 web forms (vendor enrollment, contact); referrals and procurement responses happen offline.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the MCP artifact here is entirely net-new.
scorecard_notes.ownership: A convening office: depends on DOC/NYPD/DCJS data and a vendor CMS host; owns little of its own data.

apis_observed.note: Undocumented but fully functional — returns programs (26), solicitation (25), notice (26), reports (26), data_reports (109), briefs (54), data_stories (6), team (92) as JSON. Not designed or published as an API.
The only write path — generic form-to-email (vendor enrollment, contact). Not a transactional/data API.
Exactly ONE MOCJ dataset: Supervised Release Dockets (atne-2dki). SODA /resource/atne-2dki.json.
Thin stub on the NYC.gov chassis (Akamai, nginx, Dynatrace/mPulse); most subpages 404 — the real site moved to criminaljustice.cityofnewyork.us.
MOCJ's System Data page links OUT to DOC Daily Population in Custody, NYPD CompStat 2.0, NYS DCJS, and Board of Correction for the jail/crime numbers it analyzes.
cf-ray, __cf_bm; fronts the WP Engine origin.

fruit.name: MOCJ Programs
fruit.notes: Community programs MOCJ funds/convenes, tagged by Issue and by Sequential Intercept stage (0-5). Reachable only as WordPress content + the accidental wp/v2 REST API. No Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Supervised Release Dockets
fruit.notes: The ONE MOCJ dataset on Open Data. One row per court docket released to Supervised Release; CourtCase-adjacent grain. Last updated 2023-06-12.

fruit.name: Jail population & system indicators
fruit.notes: MOCJ's System Data page: jail-population-increase and re-arrest explainers as PDFs, plus outbound links to DOC (Daily Population in Custody, Monthly Admissions/Discharges, Flash Indicators), NYPD CompStat, NYS DCJS, and BOC. MOCJ analyzes but does not OWN these numbers — aggregate only.

fruit.name: Publications, reports & briefs
fruit.notes: Reports, briefs, fact sheets, scorecards, data reports, and data stories — mostly PDF/narrative. Enumerable via wp/v2 but no Open Data twin and no structured data behind most of them.

fruit.name: Procurement — solicitations & notices
fruit.notes: How MOCJ contracts with the grantee CBOs that run its programs. Web pages + wp/v2 JSON; responses go through Vendor Enrollment / Contact Form 7. No procurement API or Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Vendor enrollment / contact
fruit.notes: The only existing write path is a generic Contact Form 7 form (feedback endpoint). Stands in for the missing transactional layer.

fruit.name: Refer to a program (make a referral)
fruit.notes: The office's core function — routing a person or court case to a coordinated program along the Sequential Intercept Model — has no API. Today it happens offline (court mandate, case manager, phone) or via a generic web form. The net-new WRITE surface; referral data is sensitive and must be gated.
